slots.caption_ig:
What's a Maillard crust? The brown crust on a seared steak. The crackle on a smashed burger. It happens when proteins + sugars hit ~300°F at the same time. We score every dish on whether the crust formed properly. No crust, no points.

#ForkFox #FoodScience #FoodAnatomy #DishNotRestaurant #FoodSmart
slots.caption_fb: There's a chemical reason a smash burger tastes better than a regular one — the Maillard reaction. It's what happens when proteins and sugars hit roughly 300°F simultaneously, creating hundreds of new flavor compounds in seconds. Most restaurant burgers never get hot enough. We score every dish on whether the crust actually formed.
slots.image_gen_prompt: Photograph, top-down, of a smash burger crust forming on a flat-top grill. Macro detail. Steam rising. Soft natural light. Warm tones. No text overlay. Square 1080x1080.
slots.video_gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16, 10s. Macro slow-motion of a smash burger being pressed onto a screaming hot flat-top. Crust forming. Steam. Text overlay: 'Maillard crust. Why most burgers miss it.' Calm, editorial, no music.
